summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring ceremonial swearing-in of new police officer Maggie Sykes, promotions of firefighter Nick Messer to Lieutenant and Todd Estep to Captain, and police awards including Officer of the Year for Kyle Hauk. The board approved the purchase of a new police SUV, accepted multiple grants including a body-worn camera grant and BWC grants, passed a resolution to limit marijuana dispensaries to the one existing location while prohibiting cultivators and processors, and initiated zoning text amendments for temporary dwellings during home construction or renovation.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for February 14-27, 2024
Approved purchase of 2025 Ford Police SUV from Lebanon Ford for $46,429
Accepted donation of 12 beanie knit caps valued at $253.44 from CPAA
Accepted OCJS grant of $48,421.20 for body-worn camera program
Scheduled special trustees meeting zoning workshop for March 11 at 6 p.m.
Passed Resolution 004-2024 to prohibit medical and adult use marijuana cultivators and processors and limit retail dispensaries to existing one
Approved out-of-town travel for SRO Groves and SRO Alcorn to attend 2024 SRO Conference at $670 each
Approved buyback of two graves from Paul Shearer at $112.50 each totaling $225
Approved Resolution R003-2024 to initiate zoning text amendments on temporary dwellings
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Robert Webster effective 3-1-2024
Approved increase in revenue of $1,810 for state fire marshal's grant reimbursement
Approved increase in appropriations of $12,603.33 from BWC FEEG grant
Approved increase in revenue of $29,915.67 from BWC grant for stair chairs and hose roller
Approved increase in appropriations of $29,915.67 for BWC stair chairs and hose roller grant
action_items: Administrator Rhodes to email zoning commission and BZA about March 11 zoning workshop
Alyssa to make phone calls to zoning commission and BZA chairs about March 11 workshop
Alyssa to buy veggie tray for March 11 workshop
Administrator Rhodes to research medical marijuana cultivator operations and provide more information to trustees
Chief Myers to wish Robert Webster best of luck from the board
Chair Hodges to sign body-worn camera grant document at end of meeting
Chief Myers meeting with architects about Station 18 construction manager at risk bid spec (next day)
key_amounts: $46,429 for 2025 Ford Police SUV from Lebanon Ford
$253.44 donation of 12 beanie knit caps from CPAA
$48,421.20 OCJS grant for body-worn camera program (5 years)
$670 each for SRO Groves and SRO Alcorn conference travel ($1,340 total)
$225 total for buyback of two cemetery graves at $112.50 each
$1,810 reimbursement from state fire marshal's grant
$12,603.33 BWC FEEG grant for gear dryer, hoods and gloves (3-to-1 match, ~$15,000-$16,000 equipment for ~$3,000)
$29,915.67 BWC grant for stair chairs and hose roller
$2.5 million requested from Ohio legislature for Station 18 rebuild
$3,100 additional BWC rebate received, plus $1,900 already received, with $2,000 more anticipated in July
notable_topics: Swearing-in of new police officer Maggie Sykes
Promotion of Nick Messer to Lieutenant (effective January 28, 2024)
Promotion of Todd Estep to Captain (effective January 28, 2024)
Officer Kyle Hauk named 2023 Officer of the Year
Drug interdiction awards for Officers Davidson and Hauk
Station 18 rebuild advocacy before Ohio Senate Finance Committee in Columbus
Resolution to limit marijuana dispensaries and prohibit cultivators/processors
Zoning text amendments for temporary dwellings during construction/renovation
Purchase of new police patrol vehicle
Scheduling of zoning workshop for March 11
notes: Meeting recessed at 6:25 p.m. for approximately 15-20 minutes for refreshments prepared by Citizens Police Academy Alumni (CPAA) to celebrate the ceremonial appointments. The board discussed at length the marijuana resolution, with Trustee Hauserman expressing interest in learning more about medical marijuana cultivator operations as a potential economic opportunity, but ultimately voted yes with the understanding the resolution could be revisited. The Lady Warriors basketball team was playing Mason in Division 1 sectional finals during the meeting. Robert Webster resigned to accept a lieutenant promotion at Hamilton Township.
title: BOT 2-27-24
